title: Paul Dano on Directing His First Film and Adapting Richard Ford's 'Wildlife' | Interview (Ep. 89)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Paul Dano about directing his debut feature Wildlife, adapting Richard Ford's novel with Zoe Kazan, and the grind of getting a first film made.

films.title: Wildlife
films.note: His directorial debut, adapted from Richard Ford's 1990 novel with his partner Zoe Kazan. Carey Mulligan and Jake Gyllenhaal play the parents of a 14-year-old watching a Montana marriage fall apart in 1960; shot over four days in Montana and the rest in Oklahoma for the tax credit.

films.title: The Grapes of Wrath
films.note: One of the films he and cinematographer Diego Garcia discussed while prepping Wildlife's look — not for the period, he says, but the spirit.

films.title: Still Walking
films.note: The other touchstone he cites alongside The Grapes of Wrath, Hirokazu Kore-eda's family drama.

films.title: Short Cuts
films.note: His answer to the show's closing question. He and Zoe rewatched the Altman film recently and he was struck by how fluidly it cross-cuts between its stories.
